zh.Skänninge, Stockholm, Sweden: 瑞典斯托可霍爾姆斯亞寧
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margriet Jan Barends Van Der Dussen於1550年之前出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特德盧時，她的父親Jan Berijszn Van Der Dussen30歲，母親unknown20歲。她和Peeter Joostens Doedijns至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1593年之後。

zh-hans.Skänninge, Stockholm, Sweden: 瑞典斯托可霍爾姆斯亞寧
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margriet Jan Barends Van Der Dussen于1550年之前出生在荷兰努-布拉班特德盧时，她的父亲Jan Berijszn Van Der Dussen30岁，母亲unknown20岁。她和Peeter Joostens Doedijns至少有3个儿子和3个女儿。她去世于1593年之后。

nameMeanings.name: Van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Dussen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from the genitive form of   Busse   1.

variants.name: Bussan
meanings: Americanized form of an unidentified German surname, most likely   Bussen  .
History: Felix Bussan, born in Germany, arrived in New York in 1897, on board the Normannia. Willy Bussan, born in Germany, left Hamburg on board the Deutschland and arrived in New York in 1907.

variants.name: Busse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ewald, Ernst, Fritz, Konrad, Claus, Eberhard, Eckhard, Frieda, Fritzi, Gerhard, Helmuth.
German: variant of   Buss  . This surname is also found in Poland. Compare   Bussey  .
North German (Büsse): metonymic occupational name for a maker of boxes and containers or for a gunsmith, from Middle Low German büsse, busse ‘box, gun, rifle’.

nameMeanings.name: Margriet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Barends
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch and German: variant of   Berens  . It is frequent in the northeastern Netherlands and adjacent Lower Saxony.

variants.name: Berens
meanings: German and Dutch: patronymic from a variant of the personal name   Bernhard  . Compare German   Behrens  .

historicEvents.description: William of Orange was recognized as viceroy of Holland, Friesland and Utrecht.
The siege of Leiden between the rebels and the Spanish. 1574 The dikes were opened allowing a Dutch fleet to sail over the flood waters and rescue Leiden.

The Union of Utrecht brought together seven northern, Protestant provinces of the Netherlands against the Catholics. Known as the United Provinces, they become the foundation of the Dutch Republic. The Treaty of Utrecht was signed, marking the beginning of the Dutch Republic.
After the signing of the Treaty of Utrecht in 1579, Brabant became a regular battlefield between the Republic of the Netherlands (Protestant) and Spain (Catholic).

en.lifeSummary: When Margriet Jan Barends Van Der Dussen was born before 1550, in Drunen, North Brabant, Netherlands, her father, Jan Berijszn Van Der Dussen, was 30 and her mother, unknown, was 20. She had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Peeter Joostens Doedijns. She died after 1593.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margriet Jan Barends Van Der Dussen est née avant 1550, à Drunen, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, son père, Jan Berijszn Van Der Dussen, avait 30 ans et sa mère, unknown, avait 20 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Peeter Joostens Doedijns. Elle est décédée après 1593.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margriet Jan Barends Van Der Dussen vor 1550 in Drunen, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Jan Berijszn Van Der Dussen 30 und ihre Mutter unknown 20. Sie hatte mit Peeter Joostens Doedijns mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1593.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margriet Jan Barends Van Der Dussen è nata il prima del 1550 nel Drunen, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Jan Berijszn Van Der Dussen, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, unknown, aveva 20 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie con Peeter Joostens Doedijns. Morì dopo il 1593.

ja.lifeSummary: Margriet Jan Barends Van Der Dussenが1550年以前にオランダノオルド-ブラバントドルンで生まれた時，父Jan Berijszn Van Der Dussenは30歳，母unknownは20歳であった。彼女はPeeter Joostens Doedijnsとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1593年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margriet Jan Barends Van Der Dussen(이)가 1550년 이전 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 드루넨에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jan Berijszn Van Der Dussen(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 unknown(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 Peeter Joostens Doedijns(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1593년 이후 사망했다.

У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Peeter Joostens Doedijns. Она умерла после 1593 г.
